chapter_context.summary: Launches the grand final vision of the new temple, where Ezekiel is measured by a man with a measuring reed in his hand, detailing the outer gate, the tables of sacrifice, and the chambers of the priests.

outline.text: Ezekiel Taken in Vision to Mount and Met by Measuring Man
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Ezekiel Taken in Vision to Mount and Met by Measuring Man (40:1-4), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Ezekiel.

outline.text: Detailed Measurement of East Gate of Outer Court
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Detailed Measurement of East Gate of Outer Court (40:5-16), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Ezekiel.

outline.text: Detailed Measurement of Outer Court and North and South Gates
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Detailed Measurement of Outer Court and North and South Gates (40:17-27), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Ezekiel.

outline.text: Detailed Measurement of Inner Court Gates
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Detailed Measurement of Inner Court Gates (40:28-37), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Ezekiel.

outline.text: Priests' Chambers and the Tables of Sacrificial Slaughter
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Priests' Chambers and the Tables of Sacrificial Slaughter (40:38-49), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Ezekiel.

key_themes.title: Prophecies of Hope and Restoration
key_themes.description: This chapter forms a core part of Ezekiel's prophecies concerning prophecies of hope and restoration, illustrating God's absolute sovereignty in both justice and final restoration.

key_themes.title: The Glory of the Lord
key_themes.description: A central theological theme in Ezekiel, describing the overwhelming majesty of God's presence, its departure due to sin, and its return in future restoration.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the visions, symbolic actions, and warnings of Chapter 40, what does this teach you about God's holiness and His desire for deep, covenant integrity in your own life today?
How does this chapter encourage you to rely on the Spirit's empowerment to stand on your feet and witness faithfully in a challenging culture?

cultural_historical.title: The Exile in Babylon
cultural_historical.text: Ezekiel ministered in exile among the deportees by the River Chebar, providing a crucial priestly voice that helped preserve Israel's faith during the collapse of the monarchy and the destruction of the Temple.
